name: Meira – tour around Riotorto
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Meira traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and varied terrain, offering significant elevation changes. The region features routes with notable climbs and descents, indicating a challenging environment for cyclists. These trails often lead through rural areas, connecting small towns and natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Meira The most popular touring cycling route is Meira – tour around Riotorto, a 26.9 miles (43.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain, making it a challenging ride. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hornos de Vilaoudriz – Vilaoudriz Kilns loop from As Rodrigas, a difficult 29.3 miles (47.2 km) path. This trail includes the highest elevation gain among the routes, offering demanding climbs and descents. Local touring cyclists also love the Tunnel with holes – Iron bridge loop from Vilameá, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Meira is defined by challenging ascents, rural landscapes, and routes connecting local points of interest. The network offers options for experienced cyclists seeking demanding rides. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Meira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Meira?
A: Touring cycling routes in the Meira region are generally challenging. All routes listed in this guide are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and demanding ascents and descents.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Meira region?
A: This guide details 5 touring cycling routes around Meira, all designed for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Meira?
A: Yes, several routes around Meira are designed as loops. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017380236/meira-tour-around-riotorto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meira – tour around Riotorto&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz – Vilaoudriz Kilns loop from As Rodrigas&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40323811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel with holes – Iron bridge loop from Vilameá&lt;/a&gt;, among others.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Meira?
A: The Meira region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll encounter rolling hills, varied terrain, and rural areas connecting small towns. The broader region, particularly near the Sesimbra coastline and Arrábida Natural Park, boasts stunning Portuguese coastline, crystal-clear waters, golden beaches, and breathtaking cliffs, providing a scenic backdrop for your rides.

Q: What are some notable points of interest or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Meira, you can explore several interesting sites. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6248099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Viladonga Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Meira&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedregal de Irimia – Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4862954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de Riotorto&lt;/a&gt; are also worth seeing.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Meira?
A: The routes in this guide range from approximately 40 to 47 kilometers (25 to 29 miles) in length, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Vilaoudriz – Vilaoudriz Kilns loop from As Rodrigas&lt;/a&gt;. These offer substantial distances for a challenging day ride, but are not multi-day long-distance tours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about cycling in Meira?
A: More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Meira's varied terrain. They often appreciate the challenging ascents, the rural landscapes, and the routes that connect local points of interest, providing a rewarding experience for experienced riders.

Q: Is Meira suitable for e-bike touring?
A: While the routes in this guide are challenging, the broader Meira region, particularly around the Meira Pro Center in Portugal, is known for offering e-bike tours. This suggests that the area is well-suited for e-bikes, which can help manage the significant elevation gains found on many routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Meira?
A: Given the region's characteristics, including its proximity to the Portuguese coastline, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable ride than the hotter summer months.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Miño River?
A: Yes, you can find routes that feature the Miño River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River – Cruz de Meira loop from Meira&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that allows you to experience this significant natural feature.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Meira are characterized by varied terrain, including rolling hills and significant elevation changes. You should expect a mix of ascents and descents, often on rural roads or paths that connect small towns and natural features.
tips: XII century monastery located in the main square of Meira. A good place to eat and drink throughout your trip.
Steep climb from Meira to the source of the River Miño. It is worth the effort to see where this mighty river begins.
Typical small town in the mountains. The most notable building is the church of San Pedro located on the hills that dominate the town.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Santa María de Meira
Church of San Pedro de Riotorto
